name: KrillApp.Trigger.Button
title: Button Triggers
description: A manual trigger that fires when clicked by the user (ON_CLICK). Has no conditions or thresholds — on each click the button completes and every node listing it in their sources is invoked, receiving the button's verb (nodeAction): EXECUTE runs the observers' normal action, RESET tells them to stand down/clear. The verb cascades through the observer chain, so a RESET-verbed button acts as a stand-down switch for a whole chain. Use this for on-demand actions like toggling a pin, running a script, or sending a webhook manually.
shortDescription: Fires on user click; observing nodes are invoked with the button's verb (EXECUTE or RESET).
llmPurpose: Fires immediately when clicked by the user, invoking every node that observes it, with no conditions or scheduling.
llmBehavior: fires on user click (ON_CLICK invocation trigger)
nodes observing the button are invoked with its nodeAction verb (EXECUTE or RESET)
RESET cascades through the observer chain as a stand-down signal
llmOutputs: invocation of all nodes observing the button, carrying its verb
llmTypicalUseCases: toggle a GPIO pin on demand
stand down an alarm chain with a RESET button
llmPromptHints: add a button to run this
let me click to trigger this
llmCreationHints: Create a Button when the user wants a manual, on-demand trigger with no schedule or condition. Ideal for testing or one-off actions. Wire actions to observe the button — list the button in their sources with SOURCE_INVOKED (dropping them under the button wires this by default). Set nodeAction to RESET to make a stand-down button that clears a whole chain.

llmConnectionHints.role: manual trigger whose observers are invoked on user click
llmConnectionHints.wiring: Observed-side: actions list this button in their sources with SOURCE_INVOKED (parent-default wiring covers nodes dropped under it); the button's nodeAction verb (EXECUTE or RESET) cascades to observers.

llmExamples: Add a button so I can manually run this webhook.
I want to click to toggle this pin.
Create a manual trigger for testing.
Make a stand-down button that resets the whole alarm chain.
